Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 1, 046 [If a person's skull suffers from fever 10][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

* NA EME-šu₂ eb-ṭe-et-ma KA-šu₂ SA₅at

(o 1) (If) a person's tongue is swollen and fills his mouth, you dry, crush (and) sieve foliage of tamarisk, foliage of adāru-tree, foliage of fox-vine (and) hound's tongue. You knead with mustard juice. You anoint the top of his tongue with butter. You put (the treatment) on the top of his tongue and he will recover.

o 22

PA gišŠINIG PA gišILDAG₂ PA gišGEŠTIN.KA₅.A

o 33

u₂EME.UR.GI₇ ḪAD₂.DU GAZ SIM ina A GAZIsar

o 44

ŠIDaš UGU EME-šu₂ I₃.NUN.NA ŠEŠ₂

o 55

ina UGU EME-šu₂ GAR-ma ina-eš

o 66

* NA IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₂-su u NUNDUN-su šim-mat TUKU-MEŠa

(o 6) (If) a person's eyes, neck and lips are repeatedly affected by paralysis and they burn him like fire, this man, the demon of the lavatory seized him.

o 77

u₃ ki-ma IZI i-ḫa-am-ma-ṭa-šu₂

o 88

NA BI MAŠKIM mu-sa-a-ti DAB-su ana DIN-šu₂

(o 8) To cure him, you pound muṣu-stone, anzahhu-stone, black frit, magnetite, parzillu-stone, zalāqu-stone, lurpânu-stone, šīpu-paste, kalgukku-paste, šunû-tree, glasswort, lulūtu. You mix in cedar resin, olive oil and filtered oil. You anoint him the morning, at noon and the evening. You wrap (the treatment) in leather (and) you put it on his neck and he will recover.

o 99

na₄mu-ṣa na₄AN.ZAḪ na₄AN.ZAḪ.GE₆

o 1010

na₄KURnu.DAB na₄AN.BAR na₄ZALAG₂ lu-ur-pa-ni

o 1111

ši-i-pa IM.KAL.GUG gišŠE.NU₂.A

o 1212

NAGA.SI lu-lu-tu₂-MEŠ SUD₂ ina UŠ₂ gišEREN

o 1313

I₃.GIŠ ser-di u I₃.GIŠ BARA₂.GA ḪI.⸢ḪI⸣

o 1414

še-ra AN.BIR₉ u AN.USAN ŠEŠ₂-su

o 1515

ina KUŠ GAG.GAGpi₂ ina GU₂-šu₂ GARan-ma DIN

o 1616

* NA mi-šit-tu₂ pa-ni-i-šu₂ IGI-šu₂ i-ṣap-par

(o 16) (If) a person, a stroke (affects) his face, his eye winks, he stares day and night, does not sleep (and) cannot stop rubbing his face with honey and butter: let him repeatedly eat maštakal-plant on an empty stomach and he will recover.

o 1717

ur-ra u mu-ša₂ ur-ta!(GA-)<na>-at-tu₂ la it-ta-na-a-a-al

o 1818

ina LAL₃ u I₃.NUN.NA IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ muš-šu-da

Bottom
b.e. 1b.e. 1

⸢la⸣ i-kal-li u₂IN₆.UŠ₂ ba-lu

b.e. 22

pa-tan GU₇.GU₇-ma DINuṭ

Reverse
r 1r 1

EN₂ ul-ḫi-ši-in MIN an-ni-bi-eš₂ qu-ma-⸢a⸣

(r 1) Incantation: "(incomprehensible incantation)". (That is) the incantation formula.

r 22

ki-⸢še₃ nam⸣-gi-si-⸢sa₂⸣ mu-bi na-an-gi-si-si

r 33

⸢ḫu-ub⸣-bi-en la-ḫu-ub-bi-en

r 44

⸢ḫu-bi⸣-en-ḫu-ub-bi-en : gi-si-ir šu-uʾ-kul-lu

r 55

[sa]-⸢ra-an⸣-[ni] ku-ul-pa-nu mu-uš-ka-tab-ba

r 66

ku-⸢uk⸣-ka-ad-dal ⸢TU₆⸣ [EN₂]

r 77

KA.⸢INIM⸣.MA šum-ma MAŠKIM KA LU₂ uṣ-ṣab-[bi?-it?]

(r 7) Incantation: "If a demon has seized the mouth of a man".

r 88

DU₃.DU₃-BI SAḪAR SILA.LIM₂.MA ana A PU₂ ŠUB-ma KA-šu₂ LUḪ

(r 8) Its ritual: he throws earth from the crossroads into water from a well and washes his mouth (and) repeatedly recites the incantation.

r 99

u EN₂ im-ta-na-an-ni

r 1010

* KI.MIN ⸢saḫ-le⸣-e u₂KUR.RA na₄ga-bu-u₂

(r 10) (If) ditto: let him regularly wipe his mouth with cress, nīnû-plant, alum, aktam-plant (and) white hellebore and let him regularly wash his mouth with water from a well.

r 1111

u₂ak-tam u₂KUR.KUR KA-šu₂ ka-a-a-nam-ma

r 1212

lik-tap-[par] u A PU₂ KA-šu₂ lim-te-es-si

r 1313

ana ⸢KIN? mi-šit⸣-tu₄ ša₂ KA-šu₂ DAB.DABtu₄

(r 13) Against an affliction of stroke that afflicts his mouth, he throws earth from the crossroads into water from a well and he regularly bathes and he will recover.

r 1414

⸢SAḪAR SILA.LIM₂.MA⸣ ana A PU₂ ŠUB-ma ka-a-a-nam

r 1515

ir-ta-na-muk-ma DIN
